verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane immunoglobulin-superfamily protein expressed exclusively on spermatozoa, essential for sperm-egg fusion via binding to JUNO on the oocyte. It has its own TM anchor and a substantial extracellular Ig-like domain, but its surface display is restricted to a narrow germline lineage (mature/acrosome-reacted sperm). Per the rubric, germline/gamete-restricted surface display with its own anchor maps to tissue_restricted_surface, not yes. Not cell-state induced, not lysosomal exocytosis, not stable_surface_attachment, not dual_localization in a generalized sense — the gating signal is germline lineage restriction.
